column1:
NOTE

The tab "DocumentGroups" was built to display document groups. This tab explains all document relationships as determined by Sara and Rachel as of November 2017.

There are a few document groups included on this tab that are not and should not appear on the "DocumentGroups" tab or the website because, for instance, one document responds to another doesn't fit any of the following categories: a reprint/paraphrase/truncation.  It just references or responds to another document.  An example: Yates1892_29Feb responds to Miller1891 but does not reprint/paraphrase/truncate any of the actual content in Miller 1891
column10:
Relationship notes 


(Direct reprints, Paraphrase, References, Quotes, Truncated reprint)


*"Direct reprints" can (and often do) differ typographically but not by content, including minor changes of wording

column13:
Publication Attribution Provided for reprinted/related copy?

Quotation marks only used to indicate reprinted text when noted

column1: "(see StillSearching)" notation indicates documents that are referenced in the archive but which we could not acquire and confirm. We never found these documents, for many reasons. In some cases, citations were incorrect but were reprinted and repeated. In other cases, physical items were held by libraries or institutions but could not be lent and therefore the citation could not be confirmed or acquired.
